steps.title: Convert percentage changes to multipliers
steps.content: When a value increases by 167%, the new amount is 100% + 167% = 267% of the original, or $$1 + 1.67 = 2.67$$ times the original. When a value decreases by 16%, it becomes 100% − 16% = 84% of what it was, or $$1 − 0.16 = 0.84$$ times that amount.

steps.title: Apply both changes sequentially
steps.content: The value is first multiplied by 2.67 (the 167% increase from 2011 to 2012), then multiplied by 0.84 (the 16% decrease from 2012 to 2013). The combined effect multiplies the original value by $$2.67 \times 0.84$$.

steps.title: Calculate the net multiplier and convert to percentage
steps.content: Computing the product: $$2.67 \times 0.84 = 2.2428$$ This means the value at the end of 2013 is 2.2428 times its value at the end of 2011. A multiplier of 2.2428 corresponds to an increase of $$2.2428 − 1 = 1.2428 = 124.28\%$$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
